channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: All Confirmed & Rumored Games For Sony State Of Play 2026
item.content:encoded:
The next Sony State of Play is scheduled for June 2, and it’s being hyped up as one of the biggest yet. Not only are Sony hiring out cinemas for people to watch the show live, but the announcement for the showcase was made two weeks in advance, with a timer ticking down to the event.

It’s got fans speculating that this could be a heavy-hitting show, and for good reason, it seems! Still, it begs the question: what games are going to feature during the June 2026 Sony State of Play?

To help you prepare for the big event, we’ve collected a list of every confirmed and rumored game for the State of Play—check them all out below.

Every Confirmed Game For June 2026 State Of Play

So far, only one game has been confirmed for the upcoming State of Play, and that’s Insomniac’s Wolverine.

It was revealed alongside the announcement for the showcase that Wolverine would open the show with an in-depth look at combat and gameplay. Expect to see extended sequences and visceral, gory combat, though it shouldn’t stop there.

  • More Insider Gaming: Official Summer Game Fest 2026 Event Calendar

Insomniac have been clear that pre-orders wouldn’t open until fans saw more of the game. Well, with the State of Play undoubtedly doing so, it’s reasonable to assume that pre-orders should open once the reveal is over—who knows, we might even get information on a deluxe edition.

Every Rumored Game For June 2026 State Of Play

There are plenty of games that are rumored for the State of Play. With a runtime of over 60 minutes, there should be plenty of reveals and surprises in store, but here’s what we think has a strong chance of appearing based on rumors and speculation.

God Of War Faye Game/Original Trilogy Remake

It’s long been rumored that a Cory Barlog-produced God of War spinoff was in development at Santa Monica, and it would feature Kratos’ wife Faye as the lead. According to numerous insiders, gameplay will differ from the Norse games, with more of a focus on action, akin to Devil May Cry. Could this be the closing announcement to end the showcase with a bang?

As if that wasn’t enough, the Original Trilogy Remake was announced during February’s State of Play, and there’s every chance we’ll see more of that project. There’s God of War galore, and it’s fair to assume at least one of these projects appears.

Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet

Naughty Dog has long been a pillar of PlayStation, and aside from the countless Last of Us remakes, fans are desperate to see more of its new IP, Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet.

Other than its announcement trailer from the 2024 Game Awards, little else has been said regarding the title. With an enthusiastic cast raving about the game, it’s high time we heard more about what Naughty Dog have been so hard at work on, even if it’s still a ways away.

Final Fantasy 7 Part 3

The Final Fantasy 7 remakes have been an incredible showcase for how remakes can drastically alter familiar stories and build upon them in new and exciting ways. With the final entry into the trilogy rounding out the 1997 JRPG classic, there’s a high chance we see an official reveal during the State of Play—even though Part 3 won’t be PlayStation exclusive.

Kena: Scars Of Kosmora

Another game that was announced during the February 2026 State of Play, Kena: Scars of Kosmora is the sequal to Kena: Bridge of Spirits. The developers mentioned that their partnership with PlayStation has allowed them to expand the Kena world while staying true to the original.

  • More Insider Gaming: Best Sony PlayStation Days of Play 2026 Deals: End Date & Sale Games

It’s looking like a real step-up, and with a release window of this year, it’d be a surprise if it didn’t show up during the upcoming showcase.

New Gran Turismo

It’s been a while since we last had a Gran Turismo title, with Gran Turismo 7 releasing in 2022. Well, it’s been confirmed that a new entry into the franchise is in development, and if it were to be shown anywhere, it’s likely to be during a State of Play. The question is simply whether it’s ready to be revealed, but time will surely tell.

Phantom Blade Zero

With a September 2026 release date, Phantom Blade Zero seems like a given to appear at the State of Play. The action-RPG has a lot of hype surrounding it, and with Wolverine also releasing in September, it’s looking like a packed month for Sony.

New Media Molecule Title

It’s been a long time since we’ve heard from Media Molecule, with its last release being Dreams back in 2020, but it didn’t exactly set the world alight, with it being more of a game-creation tool.

Well, based on job descriptions, Media Molecule’s new title will feature open-world content, with a new IP supposedly attached. I, for one, am always hoping for the return of LittleBigPlanet, but anything from the Media Molecule team would make me a happy chap.

Fairgame$ Gets A Rebrand

Insider Gaming has long reported on Fairgame$, with internal playtests supposedly not quite hitting the mark. Well, a few weeks before the State of Play, Sony trademarked a new game called Break In, and it’s rumored to be a rebranding for Fairgame$. Whether this will help improve the game’s chances remains to be seen, but we could soon find out during this State of Play.

Other Potential Appearances

There’s plenty more out there that could appear during the State of Play, and there’s bound to be a few surprises in store that managed to avoid the leak plague. That said, here are a few more games that could also receive a new announcement:

  • Resident Evil 9 DLC
  • Resident Evil Code Veronica Remake
  • Rayman Legends Retold
  • Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls New Characters
  • Silent Hill Townfall
  • Onimusha Way of the Sword
  • Forza Horizon 6 PS5 Release Date
  • 4Loop
  • Horizon Hunters Gathering
  • Castlevania Belmont’s Curse

What are you hoping to see during the State of Play? Share your predictions in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, check out the following:

  • Insider Gaming Showcase—Everything Announced
  • Xbox Games Showcase 2026 & Gears of War: E-Day Direct—Date, Start Times & How to Watch
  • Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase—Date & Start Times, How to Watch & How Long it is

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/all-confirmed-rumored-games-for-sony-state-of-play-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PlayStation Reveals FlexStrike Fight Stick Release Date
item.content:encoded:
PlayStation has stated that the FlexStrike Fight Stick will release in August 2026.

The new fight stick was announced back in July 2025 for PS5 and PCs, with the blog post at the time offering players a glimpse of the features of the new fighting-games-inspired wireless controller. The latest announcement not only provides the release date but also the price, pre-order details, and availability for the FlexStrike.

PlayStation FlexStrike Fight Stick Release Date Set for August 6

A new blog post by PlayStation reveals that the FlexStrike Fight Stick will release alongside MARVEL Tōkon: Fighting Souls on August 6, 2026. The purchase will include a sling carry case and a built-in rechargeable battery. Interested buyers can pre-order the controller starting Jun 12, 2026, at $199.99/€199.99/£179.99/¥34,980 (including tax). It will be available through direct.playstation.com and participating retailers.

Along with the new fighting controller, PlayStation is releasing a 27-inch Gaming Monitor with DualSense Charging Hook in the United States and Japan on August 27, 2026. It has been priced at $349.99/¥49,980, which comes with a 27-inch QHD IPS display (2560×1440) with VRR, supporting up to 120Hz on PS5 and PS5 Pro, and up to 240Hz on a compatible PC or Mac. Additionally, the announcement also revealed that a set of Pulse Elevate wireless speakers will arrive later this year.

In other news, PlayStation saw a massive 50% drop in first-party sales between 2020 and 2024, prompting many to wonder how the firm recovers from here. Additionally, PlayStation has caught the neck of a major shovelware publisher, leading to the delisting of over 1,000 games.

Will you be purchasing the PlayStation FlexStrike fight stick once it releases this August? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about a new Sly Cooper game being hinted at. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/playstation-reveals-flexstrike-fight-stick-release-date/

item.title: All Leaked LEGO x Pokémon Sets: Set Sizes, Smart Bricks & Expected Prices
item.content:encoded:
LEGO’s huge collab with the Pokémon franchise is set to get even better, with leaks showing a wide range of products and sets on the horizon.

Initially announced last year, the first LEGO Pokémon sets were released in February, but the next drop is set to dwarf the initial launch significantly with three times as many products coming to market.

For all the details on the upcoming LEGO Pokémon sets, Insider Gaming has you covered.

All Leaked Upcoming LEGO Pokémon Sets

15 new LEGO Pokémon sets have been leaked ahead of their official reveal, 12 of which already have images available, in a drop that is set to be significantly larger than the drop in February.

While February’s drop, which debuted LEGO Pokémon sets, was geared towards adult builders, the next range is predominantly geared towards children, with 11 of the leaked sets featuring Smart Brick technology.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Pokémon 30th Anniversary Celebration TCG Classic Cards Have Already Leaked

There will be something for everyone, however, with four other larger sets, three of which contain over 1,000 pieces, while the smallest set has over 750 pieces, still larger than all but two of the Smart Brick sets.

Check out the table below for the set names, set numbers, number of pieces, Smart Brick details, and expected price ranges based on similar products. Price ranges are based on leaks or comparable products available on the LEGO store.

When Are New LEGO Pokémon Sets Being Released?

The next range of LEGO Pokémon sets is expected to be released in August, with many have a reported drop date of August 1.

Like the majority of LEGO sets, pre-orders will be available ahead of time, and unlike the fierce frenzy that there is to secure TCG products, obtaining the LEGO Pokémon sets should be much easier.

  • More Insider Gaming: Pokémon TCG 30th Celebration Complete Set List—All Revealed Cards & Teasers

The three main LEGO Pokémon sets that were released in February are still available to buy at MSRP, either directly from the LEGO Store or other retailers, while the Badge Set and Mini Pokémon Center are no longer available, as they were only distributed for a limited time with qualifying purchases, or VIP Points.

It remains to be seen whether there will be more exclusive sets in this drop of LEGO Pokémon products, but full details should be available soon.

What do you think about the next range of LEGO Pokémon products? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon, check out the winning team of the first Pokémon Champions VGC Regional, and read about the global sales numbers of the franchise.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/lego-pokemon-leaked-sets-prices/

item.title: Maverick Games Officially Reveals CLUTCH After Weeks-Long Teaser
item.content:encoded:
Maverick Games, a studio made up of former Forza Horizon talent, has today unveiled its all-new open-world, narrative-driven racing game, CLUTCH. This reveal came at the end of a two-week countdown stream, first published by Maverick Games on May 22nd.

In the run-up to the grand reveal of Clutch, Maverick Games shared images and teasers on social media, painting a picture of this ambitious venture that promises to rival Forza Horizon, which has been on a generational run of late.

Find out everything you need to know below.

CLUTCH Takes Players To a Massive Open World

Maverick Games has been working on Clutch for more than three years from a studio headquartered in Leamington Spa in the United Kingdom. Funnily enough, that’s also where Playground Games, the developer behind Forza Horizon, is based.

In recent weeks, Maverick drove a hilarious Fiat Multipla into their office, setting it up as the site of a two-week countdown that has been silently streaming on YouTube ever since. Around the same time, the team invited content creators in the racing space to get a look at Clutch, which has now been officially and fully revealed.

Maverick was founded in 2022 by Mike Brown, formerly the creative director on Forza Horizon 5, and the studio immediately set about creating a cutting-edge open-world racing title to rival that very franchise.

Here’s the first trailer for Clutch, built into the livestream build-up (note: Maverick appears to have not listed the live stream recording, but this might be remedied. Check back!)

The team stressed that Clutch has been built out of a pure love for driving, which is apparent in the trailer, revealed as the live broadcast came to an end. The intricacies of the game’s customization model mean that players can now dress up the interior of their cars as much as the exterior, adding fine details and giving their vehicles a homely, lived-in feel.

At Summer Game Fest, Maverick Games will unveil the first full, uninterrupted trailer, so stay tuned!

Do you think Clutch will be a day-one purchase for you? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that PlayStation’s first-party sales are a cause for concern

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For June 2, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, June 2, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Mammal
  • Hint 2: Native to Asia and Africa
  • Hint 3: Known for being fast and hoofed

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Rhino
  • Leopard
  • Kudu
  • Oryx
  • Cheetah
  • Giraffe
  • Antelope
  • Wildebeest
  • Zebra

Today’s Contexto Answer For June 2, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Gazelle

A Gazelle is a mammal with hoofs and large eyes that move quickly and lightly, akin to a deer.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on June 3.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: All Pokémon 30th Anniversary Celebration TCG Classic Cards Have Already Leaked
item.content:encoded:
Excitement levels are through the roof for Pokémon TCG fans following the long-awaited reveal of the 30th anniversary Celebration set, and while only a handful of cards have been officially revealed, the full line-up for the classic reprints seems to have been uncovered.

Similarly to the 25th anniversary set, the Pokémon 30th Celebration TCG set will contain reprints of selected cards from across the three decades of the franchise, each having a 30th anniversary stamp, with base set Charizard and Pikachu & Zekrom GX having been officially revealed.

However, a teaser trailer in February highlighted some of the other cards expected to feature, and now the likely list for all 30 classic cards in the set has been detailed.

Pokémon 30th Celebration TCG Classic Cards Leaked

The ever-reliable Pokebeach has uncovered the full classic collection line-up, having painstakingly analysed the cards that quickly flash across the screen when visiting Japan’s 30th Celebration website.

Cards like the Crystal Lugia from Aquapolis, Palkia Lv. X from Great Encounters, and Uxie from Legends Awakened, which were already teased in February’s initial trailer, feature once again, alongside some other stand-out editions.

The 30 cards cover every major era from the Pokémon TCG, including Misty and Erika’s Jigglypuff from Gym Heroes, Shining Celebi from Neo Destiny, Dark Tyranitar from EX Team Rocket Returns, and Gengar Prime from HGSS Triumphant, alongside a host of more recent cards.

Magikarp’s Illustration Rare from Paldea Evolved is the most recent card from the TCG set for a reprint, giving fans another opportunity to chase the standout Shinji Kanda artwork, with Arceus VStar, Mew VMAX, Raikou Amazing Rare, and Zacian V all featuring from the Sword and Shield era of the TCG.

The 30th Celebration set will be significantly larger than the 25th set, however, which contained 25 cards in the base set, alongside 25 additional classic reprints, with set numbers showing the English version will contain a whopping 128 cards before Secret Rares.

This includes 30 different Pikachu cards, all illustrated by different artists, and a host of new Illustration Rares featuring the likes of Lapras, Hisuain Zorua, and Lycanroc.

What do you think about the selection of classic cards in the 30th Celebration TCG set? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon, check out the winning team of the first Pokémon Champions VGC Regional, and read about the global sales numbers of the franchise.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: New Crimson Desert Roadmap Update Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Pearl Abyss has unveiled a new roadmap for Crimson Desert that outlines updates and plans that will run through until September 2026.

Since Crimson Desert was released back in March this year, Pearl Abyss has practically reshaped the game from how it was at launch. Updates to difficulty settings and endgame content stood out, but it doesn’t seem like the developers are done yet, as a new batch of updates is on the horizon.

Details On Crimson Desert’s New Roadmap

The new roadmap for Crimson Desert was shared on the latest Dev Update by Pearl Abyss, and it seems to cover practically every aspect of the game. Here’s the full list of features that will be improved upon in the next few months:

  • Story Improvements
  • Re-Blockade Update
  • New Combat Content
  • Cross-Save
  • Damiane & Oongka Gameplay Improvements
  • QOL Improvements For Non-Combat Content

Main details include improving the coherence of key narrative scenes to strengthen the flow of Kliff’s story, allowing Damiane and Oongka to get a better share of the spotlight gameplay-wise, and adjustments to trading and farming.

It’s worth noting that there’s no date-specific roadmap for these updates; instead, they’ll be rolled out gradually from June through September. Pearl Abyss are aiming for these updates to be meaningful for both new and returning players, and more in-depth details will be shared as and when they occur.

To round out the post, Pearl Abyss shared that DLC is officially in development, though details on that remain incredibly scarce. Still, it’s confirmation of what was originally a consideration, so expect even more Crimson Desert content in the more distant future.

What do you make of the Crimson Desert roadmap? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about the controversial arrival of The Burnt Peanut in Fortnite, as well as the concerning sales of PlayStation’s first-party titles.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Crimson Desert DLC Is Officially In Development
item.content:encoded:
Pearl Abyss has outlined what the future looks like for Crimson Desert through the months of June to September, and amid a host of future title updates, DLC is officially in development.

It was just a few weeks ago when Pearl Abyss revealed that it was considering DLC for Crimson Desert, as new ways to broaden the game were being explored. Well, just a few weeks later and it’s been confirmed, so the studio must have settled on an idea to run with.

What Could Crimson Desert DLC Look Like?

Other than the DLC being in development, not much else is known about the future expansion. Pearl Abyss stated in its recent Dev Update that details cannot be shared just yet, and that we will have to wait for more news in the future, likely after they complete the new roadmap that runs through until September.

That being said, it was mentioned that the DLC will be a ‘meaningful addition to your journey,’ though that can be construed in a variety of ways. It could be a new quest line, a new area to explore, or perhaps even a new playable character; the possibilities in a game as large as Crimson Desert are practically endless.

It’s also unclear if the DLC will be a paid piece of content. It’s likely that it will be, given the distinction between it and the prior game updates, which included changes to difficulty settings and endgame content. How much it could cost depends on the size of the update, leaving even more speculation in the air.

Are you excited for Crimson Desert DLC? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about the controversial arrival of The Burnt Peanut in Fortnite, as well as the concerning sales of PlayStation’s first-party titles.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Noob Tower Defense Codes (June 2026)
item.content:encoded:
Use these Roblox Noob Tower Defense monthly redeem codes to get your hands on plenty of free rewards, including Gems and much more.

Noob Tower Defense is a popular Roblox game that drops players into the middle of a fast-paced tower defense world filled with waves of blocky invaders to defeat and valuable loot to discover. Players are required to build defensive towers and hold the line against endless waves of noobs upon launch, and from there, progress through the game to get the best towers and upgrades available. With enchantment systems, special tower abilities, and challenging difficulty levels on offer, the Roblox game has something for everybody.

Players progress by tackling increasingly difficult waves and map encounters, which drop coins and materials for purchasing new towers. The game features various tower types with different attack patterns and special abilities, letting players customize their defense strategy to their preferences. Players can earn cash in-game for damaging and killing enemies or by using specialized towers like the Farm tower.

Having been released in May 2021 by Hardware Productions, Noob Tower Defense has gradually gained traction over the years and now boasts a fairly dedicated player base with over 4.5 million visits. Although simply participating in the various waves the game offers is enough for players to keep upgrading their towers, using these Roblox redeem codes to get some extra gems and resources is never a bad idea.

Active Noob Tower Defense Codes

  • Likes6k – 300 gems
  • likes7k – 300 gems
  • LIKES5K – 300 gems
  • hallsecret – 300 gems
  • UPD2 – 300 gems

Expired Noob Tower Defense Codes

  • LIKES2K
  • LIKES500
  • SHIFTLOCK
  • 100KVISITS

How To Redeem Noob Tower Defense Codes

Redeeming codes in Noob Tower Defense is relatively simple, unlike other Roblox games. All players have to do is follow the steps below to redeem their codes.

  1. Open Noob Tower Defense in Roblox
  2. Move your mouse/cursor towards the left side of the screen to find Settings
  3. The box to enter codes is right at the top
  4. Click on the box and type or paste the exact code in the box
  5. Hit the redeem button to claim your freebies

Where To Get More Noob Tower Defense Codes

Noob Tower Defense codes are usually shared through the game’s official community and developer channels rather than appearing directly in-game. The quickest way to find them is through the official Noob Tower Defense Discord server, where dedicated channels regularly post new codes and update leaks, along with the game’s Roblox page, where developer Vitan Games highlights milestones and upcoming changes.

Most codes are released around key milestones, with new ones often tied to every 500 likes, as well as the weekly Friday updates and occasional holiday events. Because there’s no built-in notification system, it’s easy to miss out on active codes if players aren’t checking consistently. A more reliable approach is to bookmark this page and revisit it frequently.

That’s because this page is updated as soon as new codes are available, and expired ones are removed quickly, ensuring that only working codes are listed at any given time.

Enjoying our Roblox games codes coverage? Check out the codes for popular games like Steal A Brainrot, Radiant Residents, and Fix It Up on Insider Gaming!

Interested in the latest developments in the world of gaming and esports? Then join the Insider Gaming Discord server and engage with the passionate community.

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Reign Piece Codes (June 2026)
item.content:encoded:
Use these Roblox Reign Piece monthly redeem codes to get your hands on plenty of free rewards, including Race tokens, crystals and much more.

Reign Piece is all about sailing and buying different Races to progress. This One Piece-inspired adventure lets you captain your ship, defeat powerful bosses, hunt legendary treasures, and conquer islands with friends, all within the Roblox landscape. Advancement hinges on becoming a stronger pirate—collecting better weapons, unlocking new races, and improving your combat abilities as you progress. However, if you want faster progression without heavy grinding and Robux splashing, redeeming the active Roblox Reign Piece codes is the best way to go.

These promo codes reward you with Yen, Race Tokens, and Crystals. Yen helps you build a strong economy early on, while Race Tokens can, if luck is on your side, unlock powerful character races. While these resources can be accumulated through regular gameplay, the fastest way to jump ahead is by redeeming promotional codes.

Active Reign Piece Codes

  • REQSMET! (NEW)
  • SDSUPDATE (NEW)
  • PRIDEVSLOVE (NEW)
  • 1500CCU!
  • HUGEUPDATE
  • AIZENANDSHADOW
  • FACTIONS
  • STARRKUPDATE
  • PRIMERAESPADA
  • SORRYFORSLIGHTDELAY
  • ULQUPDATE
  • GOATOFTHEESPADAS
  • AKAZAUPDATE
  • 750INTERESTED
  • AKAZASOON
  • GYUTARO
  • SLASHERDEMON
  • BERUUPD
  • THESUPREMEANTKING
  • DOUBLEDUNGEON
  • SORRYFORSJWDELAY
  • SUNGJINWOO
  • BLESSEDUPBYSLCTHEGOAT
  • IGRISUPDATE
  • FREEREROLLS
  • MAHORAGA
  • 500INTERESTED
  • NERFMAHORAGAPLSSSSS
  • 1KINTERESTED
  • NOONEDOESITLIKEREIGNPIECEDOES
  • RYOMENSUKUNA
  • HUGEUPDATEJJK
  • REANIMATEDTOJI
  • VOWS
  • CHIMERA
  • HXHUPDATE
  • KINGSLC

Expired Reign Piece Codes

  • SOON2026
  • RELEASE

How To Redeem Reign Piece Codes

Follow these simple steps to redeem the codes:

  1. Boot up Reign Piece on Roblox
  2. Find Settings on the left hand-side of the screen and click on it
  3. Type in or copy and paste the active code into the code text box
  4. Click on enter to claim the freebie

How To Get More Reign Piece Codes

Reign Piece codes are typically released through the game’s community and developer channels, but they don’t appear automatically in-game. The best places to catch them early are the official REIGN : STUDIOS Discord server, which has dedicated announcement channels where all new codes are posted first, and the R : S TEAM REIGN PIECE Roblox Group where they announce updates and milestones.

Codes often come out around game milestones, major updates, special occasions like holidays, and after unexpected server downtime, but if you’re only checking sporadically, you’ll very easily miss them because no alert system tells players when a code drops—or when it stops working. Because of this, the easiest strategy is to save this guide in your browser and check back instead of trying to monitor every source yourself.

This page is refreshed as soon as new codes go live, and any expired codes are removed promptly, so you always see only the ones that are still active.

Having fun using codes in Roblox games? Then check out the latest codes for 99 Nights in the Forest, Type Soul, and Azure Latch. Also, check out the Insider Gaming Discord server.

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/roblox-reign-piece-codes/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Valve Tried To Kick Rainbow Six Siege Off Steam After Pricing Dispute, It’s Reported
item.content:encoded:
It’s no big secret that Valve, via Steam, has an iron-fisted grip over the bulk of the PC games market. It’s the dominant platform in the space, and that allows it to have a monopoly of sorts, easily outpacing competing launchers and storefronts.

In recent years, lawsuits challenging these monopolies have become commonplace, and Valve is no stranger to them, as the firm’s suite of lawyers is kept busy fending them off. However, a recent report revealed just how aggressive Valve can be (and has been) when it comes to ‘defending’ that monopoly.

Ubisoft and Valve Going Head to Head

In an all-new report from Bloomberg, it was explained that Valve once clashed with Ubisoft over Rainbow Six Siege. This was a few years back, when Ubisoft was still using the UPlay app on PC. It has since been rebranded as Ubisoft Connect, a replacement that occurred in 2020.

Rainbow Six Siege had been on the market for a few years by that point, and Ubisoft listed a particularly tasty deal for the game on the UPlay platform. Per the report, Valve discovered this and made a bold threat, demanding that Ubisoft remedy that price point or face the tech titan’s wrath.

That wrath would have come in the form of a complete delisting of Rainbow Six Siege from the Steam platform, the primary app of choice for PC-based gamers. It was claimed that Valve gave Ubisoft 24 hours to sort the situation out.

Thanks to GamesRadar for the spot.

Steam is still a huge platform for Rainbow Six Siege, which boasted a peak player count in the last 24 hours (at the time of writing) of 75,000 users. In general, Rainbow Six Siege is a monumental cash cow for Ubisoft, and in 2024, it was revealed that it alone had made almost more money than any other Ubisoft franchise ever made.

In response to recent accusations regarding this Steam monopoly, Gabe Newell, co-founder of Valve and eclectic billionaire, said, while suggesting that players still have plenty of options:

(Customers have enormous choice) where they purchase their products, whether they buy the game on an Xbox, whether they buy it on Steam, whether they buy it on Epic Games Store, or whether they buy it directly from software developers.

Do you think Valve needs to own up to these monopolization claims, or is it a load of fuss over nothing? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that TheBurntPeanut is embroiled in a controversy again

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: TheBurntPeanut’s Fortnite Arrival Slammed by Gamers as Pride Month Kicks Off
item.content:encoded:
TheBurntPeanut, a divisive VTuber who masquerades as an animated peanut, is making his way into Fortnite as an all-new Sidekick. His imminent arrival has been judged harshly by critics, mostly because Epic Games has given him legs and arms and made him look weird as all hell, but also because many disagree with his more controversial, historic moments.

Clips have started resurfacing across social media of TheBurntPeanut uttering slurs and promoting scandalous stream titles, all of which have been dredged up right at the start of Pride Month.

TheBurntPeanut is headed to Fortnite as a Sidekick, proving how far this animated streamer has come. In the last year and a half, TheBurntPeanut has exploded in the streaming space, racking up a staggering array of followers, coining some irritatingly persistent phrases, and spawning a cacophony of copycat creators.

Unfortunately, his impending arrival, which is scheduled to take place on June 5, has been heralded by many as a foul move from Epic Games.

In a post on Resetera, users started discussing the ‘slur-using’ Peanut, who has been captured in the past mistakenly uttering the ‘f-word’ on stream while playing Escape from Tarkov.

It’s a pretty old clip, but as recently as three weeks ago, TBP hosted one of his regular streams with other creators dubbed ‘Slur Saturday’ on YouTube. Here’s that full stream, in case you wanted a taste of TheBurntPeanut’s content:

Users on Resetera were quick to lambast Epic Games for including TheBurntPeanut in Fortnite. One wrote, ‘Streamers shouldn’t even be on BP in the first place, real people can f*** up at any moment and then you got a skin you can’t remove from the game.’ Another said, ‘Oh, gross, this was the streamer Bungie gave exclusive stream rights to a while back before Marathon came out. That f-slur one is so blatant. Agh.’

One user explained how mods over on the Fortnite subreddit appear to be ‘covering up’ the hate:

Mods are removing posts about this on the Fortnite Reddit, even with the majority of comments in agreement that he sucks.

Do you think this is a lot of fuss about nothing, or are people right to be concerned about TheBurntPeanut’s arrival in Fortnite? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Icebreaker’s raid timer has been increased in Tarkov

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: PlayStation’s First-Party Sales Collapse Paints a Picture of Concern
item.content:encoded:
In a recent data deep dive, it was explained that, between FY2020 and FY2024, PlayStation’s first-party software sales plummeted by 30 million copies. The sales figures peaked around 2020, with the label securing a whopping 58.4 million first-party sales within 12 months.

By FY2024, that figure collapsed, falling to around 29 million, hampered by a poor release slate and massively underperforming games, like Concord. It’s relatively easy to explain some of these numbers, but with PlayStation’s next State of Play arriving later today, folks are wondering what else the titan has in the bank.

PlayStation 5’s First-Party Sales Aren’t Doing So Hot

In a study published by Game File’s Stephen Totilo, the last six years of financial data were collated and represented in a neat graph, showcasing PlayStation’s first-party performance of late.

Here’s a snapshot of the high-level figures:

  • FY2018: 54.1 million sales
  • FY2019: 49.2 million sales
  • FY2020: 58.4 million sales
  • FY2021: 43.9 million sales
  • FY2022: 43.5 million sales
  • FY2023: 39.7 million sales
  • FY2024: 28.9 million sales
  • FY2025: 32.1 million sales

The sizeable peak secured in FY2020 came courtesy of a few bangers, namely the release of the PS5 console. It also welcomed games such as The Last of Us Part II, Miles Morales, and Ghost of Tsushima, all of which flew off the shelves. Let’s also not forget that it was the fabled year of COVID-19’s global lockdown trend, forcing gamers into their homes with nothing but time on their hands.

The Last of Us Part II, of course, became the gift that kept on giving, with re-releases landing later.

But things remained fairly stable out of the back of that window until 2024 came around.

From FY2020 to FY2024, PlayStation saw a drop of around 50% in first-party game sales, a figure that only recovered slightly last year with the launches of Ghost of Yotei and Death Stranding 2.

Many feel that PlayStation’s push in recent years towards studio acquisitions and live-service titles has been a wasted venture. It’s an initiative that has almost entirely fallen flat, and it has likely cost the company hundreds of millions of dollars, if not billions.

The latest entry to that discussion point has been the 2022 acquisition of Bungie, which Sony completed for around $3.7 billion. In May, it was revealed in Sony’s latest financials that the Bungie buyout had brought about an impairment loss of around $765 million, and that figure continues to grow as Bungie backslides.

Last month, the team revealed that Destiny 2 would be receiving its final update on June 9th, with the studio focusing instead on Marathon, the dwindling extraction shooter, and the ‘incubation’ of future projects.

That’s why all eyes are now on the PlayStation State of Play showcase, set to drop later today, as I write this. It’s the next big thing on the PlayStation horizon, and the hope is that it’ll yield some juicy gems that the label can rely on to drive sales back up. Sony has gone all-in on this State of Play, hosting theatre streams, promoting it long in advance, and pushing for some serious hype.

Stay tuned to Insider Gaming for the latest on the State of Play as it drops later today, and be sure to join the conversation on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that 1047 Games has revealed the first trailer for EMPULSE

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.
